channel.title: Reading-List on Marginalia
channel.description: Recent content in Reading-List on Marginalia

item.title: Comprehensive Marxist Literature (1840--2025)
item.description:
Comprehensive Marxist Literature (1840–2025) Link to heading

1. Executive Overview Link to heading

Objectives: This report compiles a complete, hierarchically structured reading list of Marxist literature from the 1840s through 2025. It spans every geographical region, ideological current, medium, and major scholarly language. The goal is to present an annotated bibliography (~15,000–20,000 words) that is chronologically and thematically organized, with transparent methodology notes for reproducibility. By “Marxist literature,” we mean the body of doctrine developed by Karl Marx and Friedrich Engels and expanded by subsequent thinkers, encompassing not only political economy and philosophy but also later interpretations, debates, and cultural expressions[1]. This includes primary theoretical works by Marx, Engels, and their successors, as well as significant secondary analyses and even creative or multimedia works that have become canonical in Marxist discourse. The selection principles prioritize works that had major influence on Marxist theory or practice, represent key ideological strands (e.g. Leninism, Western Marxism, Maoism, etc.), or mark innovations and debates within Marxism.

item.title: Comprehensive Marxist Literature (1840--2025): An Annotated Bibliography and Research Guide
item.description: Comprehensive Marxist Literature (1840–2025): An Annotated Bibliography and Research Guide
